records.ety: [Gr. <?/ to give besides; <?/ over + <?/ to give: cf. F. pidote. So named from the enlargement of the base of the primary, in some of the secondary forms.]

defs: A mineral, commonly of a yellowish green (pistachio) color, occurring granular, massive, columnar, and in monoclinic crystals. It is a silicate of alumina, lime, and oxide of iron, or manganese.
notes: ☞ The Epidote group includes ordinary epidote, zoisite or lime epidote, piedmontite or manganese epidote, allanite or cerium epidote.
